domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should courseName be modeled as a separate class associated with Course, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Course name is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual course name properties.
2. Course name is modeled as a simple representation in Course. This is suitable when course name details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Course name is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model course name as a simple representation in Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: Model course name as a simple representation in Course.

questions.question:
Should the course name in course be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use course name as string attribute in course, which allows any text value.
2. Use course name as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology or computer science.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for courseName.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration courseName with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for courseName.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with Course?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a course can exist without any associated assignment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a course must have at least one associated assignment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Course can exist without Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Course must have at least one Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Course must have at least one Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with Course?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a course is associated with exactly one assignment.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a course can be associated with multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each course links to one assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each course can link to multiple assignments.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each course links to one assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with TeachingAssistant?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a teaching assistant can exist without any associated assignment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a teaching assistant must have at least one associated assignment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant can exist without Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant must have at least one Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant can exist without Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with TeachingAssistant?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a teaching assistant is associated with exactly one assignment.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a teaching assistant can be associated with multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant links to one assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant can link to multiple assignments.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant can link to multiple assignments.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Enrollment in the relationship with Student?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a student can exist without any associated enrollment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a student must have at least one associated enrollment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Student can exist without Enrollment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Student must have at least one Enrollment.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Student must have at least one Enrollment.

questions.question:
Should Enrollment be modeled as an association class linking Course and Student, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Enrollment exists only as part of the relationship between Course and Student. A enrollment cannot exist without the link between Course and Student.
2. Regular class: Enrollment is a class that has links to Course and Student. In this case multiple enrollments can exist for every Course and Student.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Course and Student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Enrollment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Course and Student.

questions.question:
Should Assignment be modeled as an association class linking TeachingAssistant and Course, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Assignment exists only as part of the relationship between TeachingAssistant and Course. A assignment cannot exist without the link between TeachingAssistant and Course.
2. Regular class: Assignment is a class that has links to TeachingAssistant and Course. In this case multiple assignments can exist for every TeachingAssistant and Course.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking TeachingAssistant and Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Assignment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 2: Regular class, Assignment as separate class with two associations.
